chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْمُحْرِمِ يُقَبِّلُ امْرَأَتَهُ وَ يَنْظُرُ إِلَيْهَا بِشَهْوَةٍ أَوْ غَيْرِ شَهْوَةٍ أَوْ يَنْظُرُ إِلَى غَيْرِهَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 104 – The one in Ihraam kisses his wife and he looks at her with desire, or without desire, or he looks at someone else

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن أو موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/356)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن أو موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/356)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ رَجُلٍ يَسْمَعُ كَلَامَ امْرَأَةٍ مِنْ خَلْفِ حَائِطٍ وَ هُوَ مُحْرِمٌ فَتَشَهَّى حَتَّى أَنْزَلَ قَالَ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ شَيْ‏ءٌ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Wuheyb Bin Hafs, from Abu Baseer who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about a man who heard the speech of his wife from behind a wall and he was in Ihraam. So he desired her until he discharged. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is nothing upon him’.
en.sarwar: 10. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Wuhayb ibn Hafs from abu Basir who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who in the state of Ihram, hears a woman speaking from behind the wall and because of lust he discharges semen. He (the Imam) said, ‘There is nothing on him.’”
